summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI config to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js collects developer-identifying data and POSTs it to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI environment variables, GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL / EMAIL, and the contents of ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — the GitHub CLI authentication store, which contains OAuth tokens. The script extracts user/email fields via regex from these files and ships them as plaintext JSON labeled 'anonymous diagnostics', which is false: the payload includes real SCM email, GitHub login, hostname, username, and project path. The package additionally impersonates the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem: name `ai-sdk-helpers`, homepage `ai-sdk.guide`, author `AI SDK Guide <hello@ai-sdk.guide>`, and src/index.ts re-exports from the legitimate `ai` package as cover. The harm fires automatically on every install of any consumer that adds this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
